untranslatable_reason: This signal requires order flow microstructure data (counterparty identification, consecutive same-side trades from specific participants, participant behavior tracking across trades). The allowed indicator set contains only price, volume, and technical indicators—no order book depth, trade-by-trade participant IDs, or time-series tracking of individual counterparty behavior. Specifically missing: (1) counterparty/participant identifier tracking, (2) consecutive same-side trade detection logic, (3) order size comparison to rolling participant average (not market average), (4) time-since-last-order from specific participant. Vol_ratio_20 is a proxy for size relative to average, but cannot distinguish same-side patterns or identify individual participant orders.
translation_notes: Signal describes a sophisticated order flow exploitation strategy based on counterparty behavioral persistence. While vol_ratio_20 > 0.75 captures the 'large order relative to average volume' component, the core logic—detecting 2+ consecutive same-side trades from an identified counterparty within a rolling 10-trade window—cannot be expressed with the available indicators. This requires order-level granularity and participant tracking that technical indicators do not provide. A practical backtest implementation would need direct access to order book data, trade IDs, and counterparty classification.
